Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. A Visual Introduction to Machine Learning (2015) 240 upvotes, 19 comments
  2. Human Organ Atlas 61 upvotes, 4 comments
  3. Examples for the tcpdump and dig man pages 69 upvotes, 7 comments
  4. Zipp 2001 Restoration 13 upvotes, 0 comments
  5. Learning athletic humanoid tennis skills from imperfect human motion data 12 upvotes, 0 comments
  6. Rack-mount hydroponics 295 upvotes, 74 comments
  7. IBM, sonic delay lines, and the history of the 80×24 display (2019) 52 upvotes, 11 comments
  8. Show HN: Signet – Autonomous wildfire tracking from satellite and weather data 79 upvotes, 21 comments
  9. Grandparents are glued to their phones, families are worried [video] 18 upvotes, 2 comments
  10. Separating the Wayland Compositor and Window Manager 39 upvotes, 8 comments
  11. Kniterate Notes 35 upvotes, 8 comments
  12. Generating All 32-Bit Primes (Part I) 56 upvotes, 17 comments
  13. Show HN: GDSL – 800 line kernel: Lisp subset in 500, C subset in 1300 21 upvotes, 4 comments
  14. A most elegant TCP hole punching algorithm 177 upvotes, 72 comments
  15. Why Mathematica does not simplify sinh(arccosh(x)) 123 upvotes, 48 comments
  16. Centuries of selective breeding turned wild cabbage into different vegetables 115 upvotes, 45 comments
  17. Allow me to get to know you, mistakes and all 263 upvotes, 119 comments
  18. Pentagon expands oversight of Stars and Stripes, limits content 146 upvotes, 63 comments
  19. Glassworm Is Back: A New Wave of Invisible Unicode Attacks Hits Repositories 90 upvotes, 37 comments
  20. The Webpage Has Instructions. The Agent Has Your Credentials 13 upvotes, 2 comments
  21. Show HN: Han – A Korean programming language written in Rust 201 upvotes, 110 comments
  22. Show HN: What if your synthesizer was powered by APL (or a dumb K clone)? 46 upvotes, 21 comments
  23. Tech companies defeat bill as AI drains local water supplies 7 upvotes, 1 comments
  24. UMD Scientists Create 'Smart Underwear' to Measure Human Flatulence 55 upvotes, 32 comments
  25. How kernel anti-cheats work 303 upvotes, 255 comments
  26. $96 3D-printed rocket that recalculates its mid-air trajectory using a $5 sensor 295 upvotes, 255 comments
  27. What makes Intel Optane stand out (2023) 89 upvotes, 73 comments
  28. The 100 hour gap between a vibecoded prototype and a working product 157 upvotes, 200 comments
  29. Treasure hunter freed from jail after refusing to turn over shipwreck gold 151 upvotes, 202 comments
  30. Hollywood Enters Oscars Weekend in Existential Crisis 47 upvotes, 120 comments

Last generated 2:00am, 16 March 2026. Source here.
